title: In a Christian Churchyard
content:
This field of stones, he said,
    May well call forth a sigh;
    Beneath them lie the dead,
    On them the living lie.
meaning: About: James Thomson (November 23, 1834 - June 3, 1882), published under the pseudonym Bysshe Vanolis, was a Victorian-era British poet famous primarily for the long poem The City of Dreadful Night (1874), an expression of bleak pessimism in a dehumanized, uncaring urban environment.
